JWST492

Sex, Gender, and Jewish Identity

PHIL236

Philosophy of Religion

Other courses may be taken with the permission of the minor advisor.

Breadth Requirement The breadth requirement ensures that students are exposed to a diversity of religious phenomena. Most Religious Studies students will complete this requirement simply by selecting from the wide variety of courses available to them. Students with particular interests (in a single approach, like Art History, or a single setting, like contemporary North America) will need to take at least one course that falls outside their particular focus of interest. Students will need to demonstrate:

1. Exposure to a diversity of religious traditions (understood to include

African religions, Buddhism, Christianity, Hinduism, Islam, and Judaism, among others), in coursework that extends beyond a single geographic area (such as the Americas, Asia, or the Mediterranean world).
